name: Banggarla

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Banggarla (bang1339).

notes: Pama-Nyungan (Thura-Yura) language of the Eyre Peninsula, South Australia, in the modern revival orthography. Retroflex consonants are written as r-plus-consonant digraphs (rd, rn, rl), the retroflex approximant as rh, dental consonants with h (dh, nh, lh) and the palatal stop as dy. The back vowel is written oo and mapped to /u/, and long vowels by doubling (aa ii). Word-initial stress and allophonic prestopping of nasals and laterals are not encoded. The dental lateral lh and trill/tap split are inferred from the series symmetry, so this is skeleton tier.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Banggarla (bang1339)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
